title: Third script model (Greek) + SOURCES.md
context: Greek is the secondary M2 candidate script, serving Greek-language learners, classical-language students, and diaspora communities. Like Cyrillic, wrong stroke order or letter forms harm learners whose script may not be well-served by existing free materials. Greek is LTR and relatively lower-complexity than Arabic/Hebrew, making it a good second proof of the non-Latin playbook alongside Cyrillic. A gate-verified open Greek font and a confirmed script-literate reviewer are blocking dependencies.
objective: Model all Greek letters (uppercase and lowercase) and relevant digits as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef, commit SOURCES.md with full provenance and snapshots, and obtain script-literate reviewer sign-off on the per-glyph checklist.
acceptanceCriteria: All Greek glyphs (uppercase Α–Ω and lowercase α–ω, plus any script-specific variants) modeled as schema-valid GlyphModel JSON with per-glyph sourceRef citing an authoritative Greek formation source.
Gate-verified open Greek font sourced (gate-002 PASS artifact attached); SOURCES.md committed with name, URL, license, retrieval date, snapshot SHA-256 + Wayback URL.
Script-literate reviewer (native/fluent Greek writer or qualified teacher) signs the per-glyph checklist confirming stroke order, direction, and letter forms match the cited source.
Sheets render in A4 and US Letter with the script-appropriate guideline system; provenance-gate green.
Model committed CC-BY-4.0 (or CC-BY-SA-4.0 if a share-alike source is used, kept separable); every sheet footer carries attribution and license text.
resources: PLAN.md section 9 M2 (third script, candidate Greek), section 8 (medium risk — script-literate reviewer mandatory)
output: data/greek/ directory with schema-valid GlyphModel JSON files, SOURCES.md with complete provenance records, reviewer-signed per-glyph checklist, and generated trace-sheet PDFs — committed CC-BY-4.0.
